panels.title: All Probes — Status Table
panels.description: Current status of every configured probe target. Status OK (green) = probe succeeded, FAIL (red) = probe failed. Sorted by status so failures appear first.

targets.expr: count(probe_success{job=~"$job", probe_type="http", proxied="false"} == 0) OR on() vector(0)

panels.title: HTTP Phase Timing (Direct)
panels.description: Breakdown of HTTP probe phases: dns_resolve, tcp_connect, tls_handshake (HTTPS only), ttfb (time to first byte), transfer (body download). Direct probes only (no proxy).

panels.title: ICMP Avg RTT
panels.description: Average ICMP echo round-trip time per target. Only populated when at least one echo reply is received. Zero or absent means 100% packet loss.

panels.title: ICMP Packet Loss
panels.description: Packet loss ratio per target (0.0 = no loss, 1.0 = 100% loss). Calculated as (sent - received) / sent for each probe cycle.

panels.title: ICMP Reply TTL (routing changes show as TTL shifts)
panels.description: TTL value from the ICMP echo reply. A sudden change indicates a routing path change (e.g. failover, new hop added/removed).

panels.title: All MTU Probe States
panels.description: Current MTU probe state per target. State: ok = path MTU detected, degraded = below expected minimum, unreachable = all sizes failed, error = probe error. Detail explains the reason (e.g. fragmentation_needed, largest_size_confirmed).

panels.title: Path MTU per Target
panels.description: Detected path MTU in bytes over time. Calculated as largest successful ICMP payload + 28 bytes (IP + ICMP headers). Standard Ethernet = 1500, WireGuard typical = 1420.

panels.title: MTU Diagnostic Events (rate)
panels.description: Rate of MTU probe diagnostic events. Spikes are normal — they occur once per probe cycle when the prober tests multiple packet sizes. Sustained non-zero values indicate probes overlapping or continuously retrying.

panels.title: Probes Exceeding Interval (skipped cycles)
panels.description: Counts how often a probe was still running when the next scheduled cycle fired. Non-zero values mean the probe duration exceeds the configured interval — consider increasing the interval or timeout for affected targets.

panels.title: DNS Result Match
panels.description: Shows whether DNS resolution results match the configured dns_expected values. 1 = match, 0 = mismatch. Only populated for targets with dns_expected configured. Useful for detecting DNS hijacking or unexpected record changes.

panels.title: HTTP Body Match
panels.description: Shows whether the HTTP response body matches the configured pattern (body_match_string or body_match_regex). 1 = match, 0 = mismatch.

targets.expr: count(probe_success{job=~"$job", probe_type="http", proxied="true"} == 0) OR on() vector(0)

panels.title: HTTP Phase Timing (Proxied)
panels.description: HTTP phase breakdown for proxied probes. tcp_connect includes proxy dial + CONNECT tunnel, making it notably higher than direct probes. The difference approximates proxy overhead.

panels.title: Proxy CONNECT Duration
panels.description: Total wall-clock time for the CONNECT tunnel establishment per proxy target, including TCP dial to proxy and CONNECT handshake.

panels.title: Proxy CONNECT Status per Target
panels.description: Current status of each proxy CONNECT target. OK = proxy accepted the tunnel, FAIL = proxy rejected or connection failed. Sorted by status so failures appear first.

panels.title: Proxy CONNECT Phase Timing
panels.description: Breakdown of CONNECT probe phases: proxy_dial (TCP to proxy), proxy_tls (TLS with proxy, if HTTPS proxy), proxy_connect (CONNECT request/response). Shown for both successful and failed probes.
